observed: The existing deployment workflow constructed cloudflare-deployment-proof.json inline without embedding the Cloudflare Pages URL authority classification.
The committed cloudflare-pages-url-policy.mjs already classifies immutable deployment origins, mutable aliases, and project-host mismatches.
A new deployment-proof builder now embeds the complete URL authority result into schema version 1.1.0 of the proof packet.
inferred: Embedding the authority result before attestation prevents later acceptance stages from silently treating a mutable or unrelated HTTPS origin as the immutable deployment identity.
proposed: Replace the workflow's inline proof-construction block with cloudflare-static/build-deployment-proof.mjs and add its test to the deployment evidence contract step.
unresolved: The workflow file has not yet been changed to invoke the new builder.
No successful Wrangler deployment output was available to exercise the builder against a real deployment.
Cloudflare environment credentials remain externally unconfigured.

verification.runtime: isolated Node.js runtime using byte-identical source and test content before commit
cases: immutable deployment plus production alias is bound as valid authority
mutable deployment label and unrelated alias remain explicit blocking evidence
missing deployment URL cannot be confused with successful proof

privacy_boundary: Contains only repository paths, synthetic URLs, reason codes, commit identities, and aggregate test results. No credentials, account IDs, private chat, personal data, or medical claims are stored.
epistemic_limit: A valid URL authority classification establishes project-scoped origin structure; it does not establish that the deployment served correct content or that scientific claims are true.
